measurements.measurement_reference: Front door ~2.1m, ~28 courses. Second-floor window ~1.3m, ~18 courses. Multiple openings allow cross-reference.

brick_analysis.bond_notes: Standard half-lap running bond throughout main facade. Consistent coursing from stone foundation to gable peak. The brick is continuous up into the gable (no siding transition).

colour.description: Warm red-orange brick typical of late Victorian Toronto residential construction. Some darker bricks scattered throughout — natural variation from kiln firing. Photo taken in warm golden-hour light which accentuates the orange tone. True colour likely slightly cooler/more neutral red. The overall impression is of quality residential brick — not the highest institutional grade but well above common.
colour.polychromatic_notes: Buff/cream stone or cast stone used for decorative window heads (segmental arch voussoirs with prominent keystones). The buff stone against red brick creates a deliberate two-tone decorative scheme. Stone quoins may be present at building corners.

special_features.description: Segmental arch window heads in buff stone/cast stone with prominent keystones. The arches project slightly from the wall face and use contrasting light-coloured stone voussoirs. Very typical of 1880s-1890s Bellevue Ave Victorians.
Buff/cream stone sills projecting from the brick face.
Prominent Victorian peaked gable at roof line in brick with decorative bargeboard. The gable contains a tall narrow window with its own arch head.
Decorative corbelling in the gable creating a stepped profile.
Full-width covered verandah with columns at ground floor. Porch roof at first-floor level creates a balcony.

condition.notes: Well-maintained Victorian semi-detached house. Brick in good condition. The warm golden light in the photo suggests late afternoon — colour calibration should account for warm bias. Chimney stacks visible at roofline in matching brick. Weeping willow tree from adjacent property partially encroaches. This is a high-quality representative example of Bellevue Ave Victorian brick residential architecture.

texture_calibration.notes: Good face-on photo in warm light. Door and multiple windows provide scale references. The stone arch heads are highly visible and will need dedicated geometry in the Blender model — they cannot be captured by texture alone.
